actionLibraryList.title: Tabletop Thoracic Rotation
actionLibraryList.context: During the rotation, avoid rushing the movement; maintain slow, controlled motion to prevent overstretching or misalignment.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749523622522.jpg","context":"1.Raise one arm with the elbow bent and place your hand behind your head."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749523625921.jpg","context":"1.During the rotation, keep your hand gently touching or lightly supporting your head.\n2.Keep your pelvis and hips stable without moving.\n3.Position your knees hip-width apart."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale during the rotation to help relax your body.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: At the maximum range, hold for one to two seconds, feel the stretch in your thoracic spine, and keep your chest open.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: During thoracic spine rotation, keep your hips from rotating.

actionLibraryList.title: Belt Bulgarian Split Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet about two feet away from the bench, facing away from it.
2.Keep your chest up, back straight, and core engaged.
3.Lift one foot and place the top of it on the edge of the bench.
4.Bend the front knee and lower until the front thigh is parallel to the ground and the back knee is close to the floor.
5.Drive through the front heel to return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the rope exit position to ground level.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Place one leg on the bench behind you and bend your standing leg.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Inhale during the downward phase, exhale as you press back to standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Press back to standing by driving through your quads and glutes to power the upward movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750669654008.jpg","context":"1.Position the belt above your hips.
2.Stand with feet apart in a front-back lunge stance."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750669658275.jpg","context":"1.Keep your back straight and core stable.
2.Keep your knees aligned with your toes as you squat.
3.Do not let your knees touch the ground."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you squat, exhale as you stand.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep the front knee aligned with your toes, avoiding letting it go past them.

actionLibraryList.title: Belt Hip Thrust
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grips to ground level.
2.Bend your knees to 90 degrees.
3.At the highest point, keep your torso, hips, and knees in a straight line.
4.Slowly lower back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the rope exit position to the height of the ground
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Compact your entire body and flex your core.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale, then thrust your hips to push the barbell off the floor. Inhale and lower back to the start.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Briefly flex and squeeze your Glutes at the top of the movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your upper back against the bench.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750670126988.jpg"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750670130421.jpg","context":"1.Drive your hips up to fully contract your glutes."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you exert force, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your glutes and hold for one to two seconds at the top.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid letting your knees cave inward.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Bent Over Row
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core engaged.
2.Hinge forward 45°–60°, keeping a neutral spine.
3.Grip the barbell overhand, hands shoulder-width or slightly wider.
4.Keep elbows close to your sides and engage your lats.
5.Pull the barbell up along your front to lower chest or abdomen.
6.Pause briefly at the top, then return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Set the machine attachment mounts to the floor position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Lean forward by hinging at the hips while keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Keep your core engaged to stabilize yourself.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your elbows tucked and engage your core, glutes, and quads for stability.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep your breathing steady throughout. Exhale as you pull the barbell up, inhale as you lower it back slowly.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your back muscles to drive your arms as you pull the barbell up. Hold for a few seconds, feeling your back muscles contract.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Depress your shoulders.\n2.Hinge forward 45°–60°.\n3.Grip the barbell overhand.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749001869394.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your elbows close to your sides.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749001873009.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while pulling up, inhale while lowering.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your upper body stable, avoiding forward or backward sway and using momentum.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Barbell Biceps Curl
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand upright with chest up and core engaged.
2.Hold the barbell with arms naturally hanging by your sides.
3.Keep your upper arms stationary and curl your forearms upward until your biceps are fully contracted.
4.Pause briefly at the top to maximize biceps contraction.
5.Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Anchor the pulley to the feet height with one handle attachment.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Start by finding a neutral spine while standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your elbows tight to your sides and avoid swaying your body back and forth.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale on your way up and inhale on your way down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Squeeze hard on your biceps to bring the forearm and biceps together. Hold a count of two at the top.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your upper arms close to your sides.\n2.Maintain a straight back with chest up and core engaged.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748312605519.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep the palms facing yourself.\n2.Bend your elbows, keeping your forearms close to your upper arms.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748312609931.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you lift, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your biceps, curling the weight until peak contraction.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your upper arms close to your sides and avoid excessive body movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet hip-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
2.Grip the barbell shoulder-width apart, palms facing forward.
3.Hinge at the hips and bend your knees to lower down, keeping your back straight and head aligned with your spine.
4.Engage your glutes and hamstrings to extend hips and knees, pulling the barbell up along your thighs.
5.Stand fully upright, tightening your glutes at the top.
6.Unlike straight-leg or Romanian deadlifts, this version focuses more on glute contraction.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the footboard end with barbell attachment.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand with a neutral spine and grab the barbell with two hands.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Don't hunch your back or hyperextend your lumbar at the top of the motion.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep your breath steady. Inhale on your way down, exhale on your way up.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your glutes and briefly hold the contraction at the top of the motion.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Keep your head aligned and eyes looking forward.
2.Maintain a straight back with chest up and core engaged.
3.Keep your knees aligned with your toes at all times.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750233568152.jpg"},{"context":"1.Avoid shrugging your shoulders.
2.Tighten your glutes and avoid overextending your hips.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750233571939.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Drive up by engaging your glutes and hamstrings, bringing your hips and knees back in line simultaneously.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Dead Bug
actionLibraryList.context: Lie supine on the machine, grasp the handles with your arms fully extended straight up, palms facing each other or down. Bend your knees to 90 degrees, with your knees positioned directly above your hips. Keep your arms stable and pointing downward, then alternate extending one leg forward and downward while keeping the other leg in place. Control the return to the starting position, then switch sides, mimicking the "dead bug" movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749453367842.jpg","context":"1.Keep your arms straight and extended vertically upward, with your palms facing each other or facing downward.\n2.Bend your knees to 90 degrees, with your knees positioned directly above your hips."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749453371890.jpg","context":"1.Alternately extend the opposite leg straight forward and downward."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you lower your leg, inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: You should feel a strong contraction in your core.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid arching your lower back off the floor, keep your pelvis stable, and ensure your arms don't drive the movement.
